rationale: A is more immediately usable because it supplies manuscript-ready positioning and recognizes the organization as the stated high-level actor. B is slightly more conservative about unspecified benchmarks and avoids A’s unsupported suggestion that the interaction is obscured when either friction is considered alone. B is also somewhat leaner, though it overstates uncertainty by treating the actor and constraint role as wholly unknown despite their explicit mention. Both preserve the essential safeguards, clearly bound claims to the stylized model, and reject unsupported empirical, magnitude, prevalence, and novelty claims.
